subject: [PATCH 1/8] mm: memcontrol: propagate NMI slab stats to memcg vmstats

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt
reviews.summary: Requested clarification in the commit message and suggested rephrasing to better explain the issue. Also pointed out a nit about code formatting, which was addressed by keeping related changes together.

2026-05-11.patch_summary: This patch series addresses an inconsistency in the propagation of NMI slab statistics to memcg-level vmstats. The current implementation, flush_nmi_stats(), only drains per-node NMI slab atomics into per-node lruvec_stats but fails to update the corresponding memcg-level vmstats. This patch fixes this issue by propagating the NMI slab stats to both per-node lruvec_stats and memcg-level vmstats.

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt
reviews.summary: Recommended resending the patch as an independent submission to the stable kernel series, with a CC to the stable mailing list.
reviews.raw_body:
On Mon, May 11, 2026 at 03:49:36PM -0700, Shakeel Butt wrote:
> On Mon, May 11, 2026 at 10:20:36PM +0200, Alexandre Ghiti wrote:
> > flush_nmi_stats() drains per-node NMI slab atomics into the per-node
> > lruvec_stats, but does not propagate them to the memcg-level vmstats.
> > 
> > This is inconsistent with account_slab_nmi_safe() which updates both,
> 
[...]
> 
> > so fix this by propagating the NMI slab stats to the memcg-level vmstats.
> > 
> > Fixes: 940b01fc8dc1 ("memcg: nmi safe memcg stats for specific archs")
> > Signed-off-by: Alexandre Ghiti <alex@ghiti.fr>

Please resend this patch as independent patch and CC stable.

2026-05-12.patch_summary: This patch series addresses an inconsistency in the propagation of NMI slab statistics to memcg-level vmstats. The current implementation, flush_nmi_stats(), only drains per-node NMI slab atomics into per-node lruvec_stats but fails to update the corresponding memcg-level vmstats. This patch fixes this issue by propagating the NMI slab stats to both per-node lruvec_stats and memcg-level vmstats.
